content.text:
I have a question Why does entire checkpoint list
entire checkpoint explain <checkpoint-id> --generate
entire checkpoint explain <checkpoint-id> , when I enter entire checkpoint list nothing apperas as checkpoint
I’ll inspect the project’s Entire checkpoint configuration and command help to see whether this is an initialization, repository, or command-syntax issue.

input.input: const r = await tools.shell_command({command:"rg -n --hidden -S \"entire checkpoint|checkpoint explain|checkpoint list|Entire\" .","workdir":"C:\\Project\\The-Golden-Hour-Opening\\soiree-planner","timeout_ms":10000}); text(r);

content.text: The key clue is already visible: this repository has no Git commits yet. I’m checking the local Entire metadata and CLI behavior to confirm whether checkpoints require a committed Git baseline.

input.input: const r = await tools.shell_command({command:"Get-ChildItem -Force -Recurse .entire | Select-Object FullName,Length; Write-Output '--- CLI ---'; where.exe entire; entire --version; Write-Output '--- LIST ---'; entire checkpoint list; Write-Output '--- HELP ---'; entire checkpoint --help","workdir":"C:\\Project\\The-Golden-Hour-Opening\\soiree-planner","timeout_ms":20000}); text(r);

const r = await tools.shell_command({command:"Get-Content .entire\\settings.json; Write-Output '--- STATUS ---'; entire status; Write-Output '--- CHECKPOINT LIST HELP ---'; entire checkpoint list --help; Write-Output '--- ENABLE HELP ---'; entire enable --help","workdir":"C:\\Project\\The-Golden-Hour-Opening\\soiree-planner","timeout_ms":20000}); text(r);

content.text:
`entire checkpoint list` shows nothing because this Git repository has **no commits yet**:

```text
branch       HEAD (no commits yet)
checkpoints  0
```

Entire is installed and enabled correctly in `manual-commit` mode. Checkpoints are created from agent work associated with Git commits, so you first need a baseline commit and then a subsequent commit containing agent-made changes.

For example:

```powershell
git add .
git commit -m "Initial project"
```

Then make another change during an agent session and commit it:

```powershell
git add .
git commit -m "Update planner"
```

Afterward:

```powershell
entire checkpoint list
```

Once the list displays an ID, use:

```powershell
entire checkpoint explain <checkpoint-id>
```

or generate an explanation:

```powershell
entire checkpoint explain <checkpoint-id> --generate
```

`<checkpoint-id>` is a placeholder—you must replace it with an actual ID shown by `entire checkpoint list`. Your files are currently untracked, so commit only the files you actually want recorded.
